description:
You are a compliance officer who must routinely audit a fleet of Linux hosts.  
    For repeatability you want a Make-based one-click solution that launches several
    inspection jobs **in parallel** and then aggregates their results into a single
    human-readable report.

    1. Create a new project directory  
       /home/user/compliance_audit

    2. Inside that directory create the following fixed sub-directories:  
       /home/user/compliance_audit/scripts  
       /home/user/compliance_audit/logs

    3. Write three tiny POSIX-sh scripts in the *scripts* directory. Their exact file
       names and the sole line they must echo to STDOUT are:

       • /home/user/compliance_audit/scripts/check_passwd.sh  
         (echoes) COMPL_CHECK_PASSWD:OK

       • /home/user/compliance_audit/scripts/check_shadow.sh  
         (echoes) COMPL_CHECK_SHADOW:OK

       • /home/user/compliance_audit/scripts/check_world_writable.sh  
         (echoes) COMPL_CHECK_WORLD_WRITABLE:OK

       All scripts must be executable (chmod 755) and must exit with status **0**.

    4. Author a Makefile at  
       /home/user/compliance_audit/Makefile  
       that satisfies every requirement below:

       a. The default target when someone simply runs `make` must be **all**  
          (declare `.PHONY: all`).

       b. Target all must launch the three individual audit jobs **in parallel**
          (i.e. a single call to make should run them concurrently via the
          `-j` mechanism or by declaring proper dependencies).

       c. Each audit job must:
          1) Execute its corresponding shell script from the *scripts* directory.  
          2) Capture the script’s STDOUT into a dedicated log file living in
             /home/user/compliance_audit/logs with the exact names below  
             (overwrite if the file already exists):

             - logs/passwd.log  
             - logs/shadow.log  
             - logs/world_writable.log  

       d. After the three audit jobs finish, a fourth target named  
          generate_report must concatenate the three individual log files in the
          following fixed order *exactly* (passwd, shadow, world) into

             /home/user/compliance_audit/logs/compliance_report.log

          The concatenation order is mandatory because the automated grader
          will check that the resulting file contains the lines:

              COMPL_CHECK_PASSWD:OK
              COMPL_CHECK_SHADOW:OK
              COMPL_CHECK_WORLD_WRITABLE:OK

          appearing **in that order, each on its own line**, with **no other text**
          before, between, or after.

       e. Target **clean** (also declared .PHONY) must delete every file
          inside /home/user/compliance_audit/logs but leave the directory itself
          intact.

    5. Run your Makefile once to prove it works.  You should end up with the four
       log files under /home/user/compliance_audit/logs containing the precise
       strings listed above, and the aggregated compliance_report.log must show the
       three lines in the exact order with newline separators.

    6. For verification, append a short run-log file called  
       /home/user/compliance_audit/run_history.log  
       whose contents must be produced by the single command (already installed):
           ls -1 /home/user/compliance_audit/logs
       running **after** make completed.  The file must therefore list exactly:

           compliance_report.log
           passwd.log
           shadow.log
           world_writable.log

       in that exact order, one entry per line, and nothing else.

    Summary of files that MUST exist when you are done:

    /home/user/compliance_audit/
        Makefile
        run_history.log
        scripts/
            check_passwd.sh
            check_shadow.sh
            check_world_writable.sh
        logs/
            passwd.log
            shadow.log
            world_writable.log
            compliance_report.log

    The automated tests will verify:
      • Executability of the three scripts.
      • Makefile targets, parallel execution capability, and clean target.
      • Exact textual contents of every log file and run_history.log.
truth:
Directory /home/user/compliance_audit/logs MUST contain four files with
    the following exact byte sequences (including trailing newline):

    1) /home/user/compliance_audit/logs/passwd.log
       "COMPL_CHECK_PASSWD:OK\n"

    2) /home/user/compliance_audit/logs/shadow.log
       "COMPL_CHECK_SHADOW:OK\n"

    3) /home/user/compliance_audit/logs/world_writable.log
       "COMPL_CHECK_WORLD_WRITABLE:OK\n"

    4) /home/user/compliance_audit/logs/compliance_report.log
       "COMPL_CHECK_PASSWD:OK\nCOMPL_CHECK_SHADOW:OK\nCOMPL_CHECK_WORLD_WRITABLE:OK\n"

    /home/user/compliance_audit/run_history.log MUST contain
    "compliance_report.log\npasswd.log\nshadow.log\nworld_writable.log\n"

    The automated grader will:
      • test `make -C /home/user/compliance_audit clean all` executes with -j2 or greater
        in the jobserver and regenerates the above files.
      • ensure that the Makefile has phony targets ‘all’ and ‘clean’
        and a target ‘generate_report’.
      • check that logs/ directory persists after clean while files are removed.
